blurb: What we can touch will not inherit what cannot fade; flesh and blood must be clothed with something death cannot soil. Paul dlares the mystery: we shall not be abandoned to decay, but suddenly wrapped in immortality like a new skin. The promise steadies every body bearing ache or limitation—your pain is temporary tailoring for an endless garment. Let these verses carry you today; the life that feels fragile now is being measured for a dwelling light cannot erode.

verses.text: Now this I say, brethren, that flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God; neither doth corruption inherit incorruption.
Behold, I shew you a mystery; We shall not all sleep, but we shall all be changed,
In a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trump: for the trumpet shall sound, and the dead shall be raised incorruptible, and we shall be changed.
For this corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal must put on immortality.
